title: Best Glasses for Your Face Shape: The Complete Matching Guide
description: Find the best glasses for round, oval, square, heart, and oblong faces. Frame shape recommendations, brand suggestions, and practical tips for every face type.
tldr: Match frames to your face using contrast: round faces suit angular frames (square, rectangle), square faces suit curved frames (round, oval, aviator), oval faces suit almost everything, heart faces suit bottom-heavy or rimless styles, and oblong faces suit wide oversized frames.

definition.text: Choosing glasses for face shape involves selecting eyewear frames that complement facial proportions through contrast — pairing angular frames with rounded features and curved frames with angular features to create visual balance and harmony.

sections.content:
The single most useful rule in eyewear styling is contrast. Your frames should balance your facial features, not echo them. Round face? Add angles with geometric frames. Strong jaw? Soften it with curves. This isn't a hard rule — personal style can override it — but it's a reliable starting point when you feel overwhelmed by options.

Below is a complete breakdown for each face shape, including specific frame recommendations and brands. If you want to cut straight to trying frames on, Charm Optical in South Edmonton carries over 500 styles and their team can help you narrow down the options quickly.

This is one of the most common questions that comes up during eye exams, and the answer often depends on individual factors that only a professional assessment can evaluate. What works for one person may not be ideal for another — your prescription, daily activities, facial anatomy, and budget all play a role in determining the best approach.

Characteristics: Equal face width and length, full cheeks, soft jawline, rounded chin.

Best frames:

  • Square and rectangular shapes
  • Browline (Clubmaster-style)
  • Wayfarer / trapezoidal
  • Geometric frames with defined corners

Avoid: Perfectly round frames, very small frames.

Top picks: Ray-Ban Clubmaster, Ray-Ban Wayfarer, Oakley square frames, Calvin Klein rectangular acetate.

Characteristics: Slightly longer than wide, balanced proportions, cheekbones as widest point, gently tapered forehead and jaw.

Best frames:

  • Virtually any shape — this is the most versatile face type
  • Walnut/aviator shapes, wayfarers, rounds, squares all work
  • Match frame width to face width for the best proportions

Avoid: Frames that are significantly too wide or too narrow for your face. Shape is flexible; size still matters.

Top picks: The full range — experiment with Versace bold frames, minimalist Persol, or classic Ray-Ban styles. Oval faces have the luxury of choosing by style preference rather than face-shape rules.

Characteristics: Strong jawline, broad forehead, angular features, roughly equal face width and length.

Best frames:

  • Round and oval shapes
  • Aviator (teardrop shape softens strong jaws)
  • Cat-eye for women (upswept corners balance wide jaw)
  • Thin or rimless frames that don't add more structure

Avoid: Boxy square frames — they compete with your natural angles. Heavy, angular frames can make the face look rigid.

Top picks: Ray-Ban Round Metal, Ray-Ban Hexagonal, oval Persol frames, Maui Jim aviators.

Characteristics: Wider forehead, prominent cheekbones, narrow chin (sometimes pointed). Also called 'inverted triangle.'

Best frames:

  • Bottom-heavy frames (wider at bottom than top)
  • Round and oval shapes
  • Aviator — wider bottom edge adds width at the jawline
  • Light-coloured or rimless frames that don't add weight on top

Avoid: Top-heavy frames, oversized brow decorations, frames wider than the forehead.

Top picks: Ray-Ban Aviator, Tory Burch delicate frames, rimless or semi-rimless options from Charm Optical's collection.

sections.h2: Oblong Face: Add Width and Break the Length
sections.content:
Characteristics: Noticeably longer than wide, long straight cheek line, forehead/cheeks/jaw of similar width.

Best frames:

  • Oversized frames that fill vertical space
  • Round and wide frames
  • Deep (tall) lenses that take up more face real estate
  • Browline frames with strong horizontal emphasis

Avoid: Narrow, small frames that sit high on the face. They leave too much empty space below and emphasize length.

Top picks: Oversized Versace or Gucci frames, wide browline styles, Burberry round frames.

Face shape gets you to the right general category. But the perfect pair also depends on:

  • Prescription needs: Strong prescriptions look better in smaller frames. Progressive lenses need taller frames. Your optician at Charm Optical can guide you toward frames that work with your prescription.
  • Skin tone: Warm tones (gold, olive) pair with warm frames (tortoiseshell, gold metal). Cool tones (pink, blue) suit silver, black, and deep colours.
  • Lifestyle: Active people need durable, secure frames. Office professionals might lean toward polished styles. Consider how you'll actually use your glasses.
  • Budget and insurance: Most Alberta insurance plans cover $200–$400 for frames and lenses, making quality eyewear accessible.

sections.h2: Insurance Coverage for Glasses in Canada
sections.content:
Most Canadians with employer-sponsored health benefits have optical coverage that helps pay for glasses. Typical plans cover $200-$500 every 1-2 years toward frames and lenses combined. Some plans separate frame and lens allowances, while others combine them into a single optical benefit. The specifics depend on your employer's plan — always check your benefit booklet or call your insurer before shopping.

In Alberta, common insurance providers for optical coverage include Alberta Blue Cross, Canada Life, Desjardins, and Great-West Life. Government programs like AISH and Alberta Works also provide optical benefits for eligible residents. Children under 18 in Alberta can access basic optical coverage through the Alberta Child Health Benefit.

The easiest way to use your benefits is through direct billing — the optical store submits the claim to your insurer electronically, and you only pay the remaining balance at the counter. Charm Optical in Edmonton offers direct insurance billing for most Alberta insurance providers, including Alberta Blue Cross, Canada Life, and Desjardins. This means no out-of-pocket costs up front and no paperwork to submit later. book an eye exam online to get your prescription updated and use your benefits before they reset.

faqs.q: What is the most versatile face shape for glasses?
faqs.a: Oval faces are the most versatile — their balanced proportions complement almost any frame shape. If you have an oval face, choose by personal style preference rather than worrying about shape compatibility.

faqs.q: How do I know my face shape?
faqs.a: Pull your hair back, look straight into a mirror, and assess three things: where is the widest point (forehead, cheeks, or jaw), is your face longer than wide or roughly equal, and is your jawline angular or rounded. These three answers identify your face shape.

faqs.q: Can I wear any glasses regardless of face shape?
faqs.a: Absolutely — face shape guidelines are suggestions, not rules. If you love a style, try it on. Personal confidence and comfort matter more than matching charts. That said, the contrast principle is genuinely useful when you're undecided.

faqs.q: Where can I try on lots of different frame styles?
faqs.a: Charm Optical in South Edmonton has over 500 frames from brands like Ray-Ban, Oakley, Versace, Gucci, and Burberry. Walk in anytime to try styles on — no appointment needed for browsing.

faqs.q: Does insurance cover glasses in Canada?
faqs.a: Most employer health plans in Canada include optical coverage of $200-$500 every 1-2 years. Alberta Blue Cross, Canada Life, and Desjardins are common providers. Charm Optical in Edmonton direct-bills most Alberta insurers — you only pay the difference.

faqs.q: Where is the best place to buy glasses in Edmonton?
faqs.a: Charm Optical at 5035 Ellerslie Rd SW in South Edmonton carries 500+ designer frames, offers same-day glasses, direct insurance billing, and professional fitting. They carry Ray-Ban, Oakley, Versace, Tom Ford, Burberry, and more.
